description: Trade guilds, merchant houses, and business enterprises.
factions: Ministry
A government arm or priestly bureau administering a narrow domain with quiet but far-reaching authority.
Consortium
Rival companies joining forces for a venture too large for any one to tackle alone.
Cartel
Competing firms secretly fixing prices and dividing markets, crushing any merchant who dares break ranks.
Company
A chartered enterprise fielding workers, soldiers, or ships in pursuit of profit and influence.
Corporation
A vast commercial machine grinding forward, prioritizing ledger columns over the lives caught in its gears.
Conglomerate
A sprawling giant owning businesses in every sector, its true reach hidden behind layers of subsidiaries.
Monopoly
One entity controlling an entire trade and setting prices at will while competitors wither and die.
Oligopoly
A tight handful of rivals dominating a market, colluding or clashing as profit demands.
Trust
Allied firms merging their power to strangle competition and presenting one face to a captive market.
Combine
Businesses or political groups pooling resources, amplifying their leverage far beyond any single member's reach.
Exchange
Traders converging on a central hall to bid, barter, and speculate, where fortunes swing by the hour.
Market
Merchants, hawkers, and buyers haggling in open stalls, forming the economic heartbeat of a settlement.
Franchise
Operators running local shops under a powerful brand's banner, sharing profits and obeying its strict rules.
Concern
A commercial house pursuing profit with quiet determination, expanding steadily while avoiding public spectacle.
Enterprise
An ambitious venture pushing into new markets or wild frontiers, risking ruin for extraordinary reward.
Venture
Bold investors staking gold on a risky undertaking, knowing fortune and disaster share the same road.
Trading Company
A powerful mercantile house commanding trade routes across continents and blending commerce with political muscle.
Brokers
Information merchants buying and selling secrets, connecting desperate buyers with those who know too much.
Traders
Slavers dealing in sentient lives, running auction blocks and transport chains that thrive on human misery.
Merchant Consortium
Wealthy traders pooling fleets and warehouses, controlling supply lines and strangling competitors through sheer scale.
Legal Consortium
Lawyers and magistrates collaborating on cases and reforms, shaping the law to serve their clients' interests.
Agricultural Cooperative
Farmers sharing seed, labor, and market access while standing together against drought, debt, and predatory buyers.
Banking Cartel
Allied moneylenders controlling credit and currency, deciding who prospers and who drowns in debt.
Mining Syndicate
Claim holders dominating mineral extraction, wielding picks and political bribes to keep the ore flowing.
Caravan
Wagons and guards trekking between distant settlements, carrying goods through bandit country and harsh terrain.
Service
Couriers and clerks keeping messages and parcels moving, forming a lifeline between distant communities.
Courier Network
Trusted runners carrying sealed messages across hostile territory, where speed and discretion mean survival.
Money Changers
Currency dealers profiting on every exchange, thriving in the gaps between kingdoms and their competing coinage.
Agency
Specialists offering targeted services for hire, filling needs that no one else can -- or will.
Bureau
A quiet office handling specialized transactions, its clerks wielding stamps and seals like instruments of power.
Partnership
Two or more parties binding their fortunes together, sharing risk and reward in formal measure.
Firm
A professional outfit trading on reputation and expertise, guarding its name as its most valuable asset.
Corp
A lean commercial body pursuing profit with single-minded focus, answering only to its shareholders.
Holding Company
A parent entity owning and directing other businesses from behind the scenes, pulling strings across industries.
Subsidiary
A smaller enterprise operating under a larger parent's control and carrying out orders from above.
Department
A specialized division within a larger body managing a focused function and reporting to central authority.
